title: Autopilot Monitor Analyze Rule
description: Defines how to analyze collected events to detect issues during Autopilot enrollment.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor IntelliSense.

description.description: Detailed description of what this rule detects.

severity.description: Severity level of the detected issue.

enabled.description: Whether this rule is enabled. Set to false to add a rule without activating it immediately.

isCommunity.description: Whether this is a community-contributed rule. Community rules are stored in the global partition alongside built-in rules.

trigger.description: Rule trigger type: "single" matches individual events, "correlation" combines multiple event types.

conditions.description: Conditions evaluated against the event stream. All required conditions must match for the rule to fire.

preconditions.description: Optional device-fact gates evaluated BEFORE conditions. ALL preconditions must pass; if any fails the rule is silently skipped — no result, no UI card. Useful for filtering out hardware/OS profiles where the rule does not apply (e.g. virtual machines).

baseConfidence.description: Base confidence score (0-100) when required conditions match.

confidenceFactors.description: Additional factors that increase confidence when matched.

confidenceThreshold.description: Minimum confidence score (0-100) to create a RuleResult.

explanation.description: Detailed explanation of the detected issue. Supports markdown formatting.

remediation.description: Steps to remediate the detected issue.

markSessionAsFailedDefault.description: When true, a firing of this rule causes the entire enrollment session to be marked as failed (KO criterion). Tenants can override this via the portal.

templateVariables.description: Template variables that must be customized per-tenant. If non-empty, the rule is a template: enabling it creates a tenant custom copy with the user's values substituted.

derivedFromTemplateRuleId.description: If this custom rule was created from a template, the original template rule's ID. Used to track lineage and prevent duplicate copies.

ruleCondition.description: A condition evaluated against the event stream.

eventType.description: Event type to match on. For event_correlation: the first event type (Event A).

dataField.description: Data field to match on. Uses dot notation for nested fields (e.g., "data.errorCode"). For event_data_array: the array field to iterate (e.g., "artifacts").

itemField.description: For event_data_array only: the sub-field on each array element to test with operator/value (e.g., "identity"). Empty = treat each element as a scalar. The condition matches when ANY element satisfies the operator.

required.description: Whether this condition must match for the rule to fire. If false, it only contributes to confidence scoring.

correlateEventType.description: The second event type to correlate with (Event B). Only used when source = "event_correlation".

joinField.description: Data field to join on — must have the same value in both Event A and Event B.

timeWindowSeconds.description: Maximum time in seconds between Event A and Event B. Null or 0 means no time limit.

suppressByEvent.description: Optional suppression: if an event of the specified type exists with the same joinField value as the matched event, the match is skipped. Used to prevent rules from firing when a subsequent event resolved the issue (e.g., app_install_completed suppresses app_install_failed for the same appId).

eventType.description: The event type that resolves/suppresses the matched event (e.g., "app_install_completed").

joinField.description: The data field to join on — must have the same value in both the matched event and the suppressing event (e.g., "appId").

eventAFilterField.description: Optional filter field on Event A (the first event).

eventAFilterOperator.description: Operator for the Event A filter.

eventAFilterValue.description: Value for the Event A filter.

rulePrecondition.description: A device-fact gate evaluated before conditions. Pure boolean filter — does not contribute to evidence or confidence.

eventType.description: Event type carrying the field to test (e.g., "hardware_spec", "os_info", "tpm_status").

dataField.description: Data field to test. Uses dot notation for nested fields.

operator.description: Comparison operator. Same vocabulary as conditions.

value.description: Value to compare against. Boolean values are stringified ("true"/"false").

description.description: Human-readable note explaining the intent (e.g., "skip on virtual machines").

confidenceFactor.description: A factor that increases confidence when matched.

weight.description: Confidence weight to add when this factor matches (0-100).

remediationStep.description: A remediation step with title and sub-steps.

templateVariable.description: A variable in a rule condition that must be customized per-tenant before the rule can be used.

label.description: Human-readable label shown in the configuration UI.

description.description: Help text explaining what value is expected.

conditionIndex.description: Zero-based index into the conditions array where this variable lives.

field.description: Which field on the condition to customize.

placeholder.description: The placeholder value that ships with the template (e.g., "CN=YOUR-CERTIFICATE-SUBJECT").

validation.description: Optional regex pattern to validate user input.
